map: DOC SET (all absolute paths, repo C:\Users\persi\Desktop\MCPenny): CONTRACT.md (1009 lines, authoritative spec; equity core §1-15, crypto perps track §16 at lines ~820-911, Hyperliquid testnet execution §17 at lines ~912-1009, live-verified operational notes §17.5 at lines ~989-1008); README.md (install/usage incl. 'Paper trade on the Hyperliquid testnet' section ~lines 285-329); plan.md (design rationale; §8 host-run bull/bear/judge debate layer, §9 sector scans, §10 research-backed edges; NO crypto/execution sections — CONTRACT §16/§17 are the only authority there); RESEARCH.md (citations; §5 crypto derivatives signal rationale, 'Caveats' = alpha decay, cross-sectional>absolute, vol-target=drawdown control only); CLAUDE.md (project guide; points to 4 memory files under ~/.claude/projects/C--Users-persi-Desktop-MakeCrazyPenny/memory/ — THOSE FILES DO NOT EXIST on disk: the actual project dir is C--Users-persi-Desktop-MCPenny and contains no memory/ — stale links); pyproject.toml; .env.example; .env. ARCHITECTURE: strict acyclic Layer 2 (orchestration/) -> Layer 1 (servers/) -> Layer 0 (providers/ behind one ProviderRegistry owning TokenBucket rate limits, TTLCache L1 mem + L2 disk JSON with single-flight, CircuitBreaker, per-capability fallback chains), plus core/ (types/config/disclaimer/errors/redact/symbols/sectors/universe/crypto_universe), analysis/ (factors, risk, regime, backtest, indicators, leverage, crypto_metrics, crypto_regime), and the deliberate exception execution/ (the ONLY authenticated state-mutating path). Registry contract: registry.fetch(capability, *, ttl=None, **params) -> {"provider": name, "data": <to_dict() JSON-ready>, "cached": bool}; MissingApiKey/NotImplementedError = silent fall-through (never trips breaker); AllProvidersFailed(capability) when chain exhausted. Equity capability vocabulary is FROZEN (§4: ohlcv, quote, fundamentals, company_news, news_sentiment, social_sentiment, congress_trades, insider_transactions, analyst_ratings, price_targets, upgrades_downgrades, sec_filings); crypto adds separate keys (§16.1: crypto_ohlcv/crypto_quote/funding_rate/open_interest/long_short_ratio -> [binance,bybit(,coingecko)], crypto_sentiment -> [fear_greed], crypto_global -> [coingecko]) — all keyless; Binance fapi is HTTP-451 geo-blocked from US IPs and falls through to Bybit automatically. DECISION ENGINE (orchestration/debate.py, pure/AI-free): gather_evidence(symbol, *, settings=None) -> dict (tolerant fan-out, failures become {"_error":...}) -> score_evidence(dossier) -> dict (weighted factors; analyst signals de-meaned vs +0.30 consensus / +10% target-upside baselines; dollar-weighted flow) -> decide_from_scores(symbol, scored, *, transcript=None, verdict=None, method="quant", note=None) -> TradeDecision (position only if net>threshold AND conviction>floor AND >=2 corroborating categories, else AVOID; a host 'verdict' from finalize_decision overrides the call but preserves quant factors) -> decide(symbol, *, settings=None) -> TradeDecision. Enrichment attaches sizing (ATR stop/target, min(vol-target, half-Kelly) position %, regime-scaled) and regime (SPY 200-DMA + 12-1 TSM + vol -> risk_on/caution/risk_off + 0..1 gross scalar). Wider surfaces: scan_sector(sector, *, limit=None, top_n=5, settings=None) -> SectorScan (MAX_CONCURRENCY=5); screen_market(*, symbols=None, shortlist=15, top_n=3, force_refresh=False, settings=None) -> MarketScreen (prefilter whole S&P500 at MAX_PREFILTER_CONCURRENCY=8, deep-dive shortlist at MAX_DEEP_CONCURRENCY=5; universe via core/universe.fetch_sp500 live->cache->fallback); build_portfolio(symbols, *, max_positions, max_weight, regime=None) -> dict (conviction x inverse-vol, iterative caps, regime-scaled gross). CRYPTO TRACK (§16): orchestration/crypto.py decide_crypto(symbol, interval, leverage_cap) reuses the equity scorers with interval-aware saturations (factor windows rescaled by sqrt(interval/1d), vol annualized sqrt(365)), adds contrarian derivatives factors (funding centered on +0.01%/8h equilibrium; OIxprice matrix; long/short ratio; Fear&Greed contrarian only at >=75/<=25), then enrich_crypto_decision attaches crypto_regime + leverage_plan (analysis/leverage.py: liquidation_price, max_safe_leverage caps leverage so the ATR stop sits inside liquidation by crypto_liq_buffer; suggested leverage = DEFAULT_LEVERAGE_FRACTION (1/2) of max-safe; funding_cost over the hold; sized to crypto_risk_per_trade), sets asset_class="crypto". Providers discover each symbol's real funding interval (4h/1h perps exist); a missing funding rate RAISES (falls through chain) rather than reading 0. core/symbols.py canonicalizes (BTC/BTC-USD/BTC/USDT -> BTCUSDT; to_hyperliquid_coin: BTCUSDT -> BTC). Screener: core/crypto_universe.py top perps by 24h volume + fetch_hyperliquid_perps (keyless POST /info {"type":"meta"}; live->cache->unavailable, NO curated fallback); screen_crypto(hyperliquid_only=True) default drops non-HL-listed symbols so the screener never suggests an untradable coin. EXECUTION (§17, makecrazypenny/execution/hyperliquid.py): HyperliquidPaperClient, a SYNCHRONOUS wrapper over SDK Info/Exchange (SDK does msgpack action-hash + EIP-712 signing). Reads: account_state (reports perp equity + spot_usdc + combined tradable_usdc), open_orders (frontend_open_orders w/ trigger metadata, falls back to plain listing), recent_fills. Writes: set_leverage, open_position (market via market_open, limit via order GTC; optional stop_loss/take_profit attach AFTER fill), set_position_tpsl (reduce-only trigger-market orders {"trigger":{triggerPx,isMarket:true,tpsl:"sl"|"tp"}} via bulk_orders under the positionTpsl grouping = exchange-side OCO bound to the position, survives client disconnect; side/size derived from live position; wrong-side-of-mid trigger REJECTED; fired market leg limit_px = trigger*(1±hl_default_slippage)), close_position (market_close), cancel_order. Validates coin against info.meta(), rounds size to szDecimals, rounds limit px to perp wire format (5 sig figs, <=6-szDecimals decimals), enforces $10 min order notional. _build_clients(settings) is the lazy-import + test monkeypatch seam (tests mock it; offline suite runs without the SDK). orchestration/paper_trade.py: async wrappers via asyncio.to_thread, tolerant {"error":...}; open_from_decision runs decide_crypto, sizes notional = tradable_usdc * plan.notional_pct (override notional_usd), leverage = plan.suggested_leverage, side = plan direction, attach_tpsl=True default places plan stop/target as the position TP/SL; AVOID/FLAT verdict places NOTHING. MCP tools: paper_pairs (keyless) + keyed paper_account, paper_orders, paper_trade_decision, paper_open, paper_set_tpsl, paper_close, paper_cancel, paper_set_leverage; CLI flags --paper-pairs/--paper-account/--paper-trade/--paper-open/--paper-set-tpsl/--paper-close. PYPROJECT (C:\Users\persi\Desktop\MCPenny\pyproject.toml): build hatchling; requires-python >=3.11; runtime deps = mcp>=1.2, claude-agent-sdk, yfinance, pandas, ta, httpx, python-dotenv. Extras: ui=[streamlit>=1.30]; trade=[hyperliquid-python-sdk] — YES, the Hyperliquid SDK is an OPTIONAL extra (pulls eth-account for EIP-712 signing; package imports and offline tests run without it); dev=[pytest, pytest-asyncio, respx, ruff, streamlit>=1.30]. Console scripts: makecrazypenny = makecrazypenny.orchestration.main:cli; makecrazypenny-mcp = makecrazypenny.mcp_server:main; makecrazypenny-dashboard = makecrazypenny.ui.launch:launch. ruff line-length 100; pytest asyncio_mode=auto, testpaths=tests. ENV: .env.example documents (names only): ALPHA_VANTAGE_API_KEY, FINNHUB_API_KEY, FMP_API_KEY, MARKETAUX_API_KEY, COINGECKO_API_KEY, MCP_BINANCE_BASE_URL, MCP_BYBIT_BASE_URL, MCP_CRYPTO_MAX_LEVERAGE, MCP_CRYPTO_RISK_PER_TRADE, MCP_CRYPTO_MAINT_MARGIN, MCP_CRYPTO_TARGET_VOL, MCP_CRYPTO_LIQ_BUFFER, MCP_HL_PRIVATE_KEY, MCP_HL_ACCOUNT_ADDRESS, MCP_HL_SLIPPAGE, MCP_HL_TESTNET_URL, MCP_CACHE_DIR. The actual .env contains ONLY these 5 names: ALPHA_VANTAGE_API_KEY, FINNHUB_API_KEY, FMP_API_KEY, MARKETAUX_API_KEY, MCP_CACHE_DIR (values not inspected). NOTE: MCP_HL_PRIVATE_KEY and MCP_HL_ACCOUNT_ADDRESS are NOT in .env and NOT present in process, User, or Machine environment scopes (existence-only check) — Hyperliquid credentials are not configured in any location I could verify; they may live in an MCP host config or be set per-session. Crypto settings defaults ('aggressive' preset, §16.7): crypto_max_leverage=20, crypto_risk_per_trade=0.025, crypto_maint_margin_rate=0.005, crypto_target_vol=0.80, crypto_liq_buffer=0.5; hl_default_slippage=0.05. DOCUMENTED DECIDE->PLACE WORKFLOW (CLAUDE.md quickstart + README + §17): 1) paper_pairs (keyless) confirm coin is a listed testnet perp; 2) crypto_decide SYMBOL or crypto_screen (already HL-filtered); 3) paper_account — check tradable_usdc (perp + spot); 4) paper_trade_decision SYMBOL (decide + place engine-sized order with OCO TP/SL) or paper_open for manual; 5) monitor via paper_account/paper_orders; exit via paper_close/paper_cancel. §17.5 LIVE-VERIFIED GOTCHAS that matter programmatically: (a) Agent/API wallet vs master account — an agent key trades on behalf of a funded master but its own address holds no balance; if signing with an agent key you MUST set MCP_HL_ACCOUNT_ADDRESS to the master address or reads (account_state/positions/fills) look empty while orders actually fill; the exchange userRole info call ({"role":"agent","data":{"user":<master>}}) reveals the master; agent wallets CANNOT move funds — usd_class_transfer/withdrawals are rejected with 'Must deposit before performing actions' and must be signed by the master or done in the testnet UI. (b) Spot vs perp collateral — USDC sits in separate spot and perp wallets; HL pulls spot collateral into perp as needed, so a flat perp account reads $0 while funds sit in spot; account_state therefore reports spot_usdc and combined tradable_usdc, and open_from_decision sizes against tradable_usdc. (c) On open_position, TP/SL attach only once the entry FILLS — a resting limit entry reports tpsl.skipped; a TP/SL attach failure never voids the entry (surfaces under 'tpsl'). (d) Hard backstops at placement: coin validated against info.meta() regardless of screener filtering; $10 min notional; szDecimals size rounding; 5-sig-fig price wire format; wrong-side trigger rejection. (e) Tools are LIVE by default — no dry-run gate; real testnet orders immediately.

extension_points.how: Add any new authenticated/state-mutating capability here, never in providers/ or servers/; replicate the _build_clients(settings) lazy-import seam so tests can monkeypatch and the package stays import-safe without the 'trade' extra.
extension_points.notes: execution/ is the spec's deliberate sole exception to read-only/keyless (§17); keep it testnet-locked — the spec forbids any mainnet base-URL field anywhere.

extension_points.how: Extend the decision-driven path (open_from_decision) or add new async wrappers (asyncio.to_thread + tolerant {"error":...} returns) for swarm behaviors like rebalancing, trailing stops, or scheduled re-decides.
extension_points.notes: This is where engine output (leverage_plan: notional_pct, suggested_leverage, stop/target) is translated into orders; sizing must stay against tradable_usdc per §17.5.

extension_points.how: Every new capability gets a deterministic AI-free tool returning a JSON string via text_result(...), optionally a host-run prompt persona; CLI flags mirror tools. Logs to stderr (stdout is the JSON-RPC stream).
extension_points.notes: Pattern set by paper_* tools and decide/crypto_decide; prompts must end with the not-investment-advice reminder.

extension_points.how: New data sources = new Provider subclass (name, supported, rate_per_min, requires_key) + new capability keys in CAPABILITY_CHAINS (env-overridable via MCP_CHAIN_<CAPABILITY>); the §4 equity vocabulary is FROZEN, so add new keys like §16.1 did for crypto.
extension_points.notes: Missing key must raise MissingApiKey (silent fall-through); only genuine runtime errors may trip the circuit breaker.

extension_points.how: The only existing always-on-shaped hook: alert configs + last-seen state persist under MCP_CACHE_DIR; sinks are console/file/webhook. A swarm scheduler could poll check_alerts and add crypto-side alert kinds.
extension_points.notes: Currently equity-only (congress + report deltas) and pull-based; no daemon exists.

extension_points.how: Swarm candidate-generation: screen_crypto(hyperliquid_only=True) already constrains suggestions to tradable HL perps; extend the funnel or universe source behind the same live->cache->fallback interface.
extension_points.notes: fetch_hyperliquid_perps is keyless (POST /info meta) and is the right pre-trade tradability check.

extension_points.how: Add pure, offline-testable risk/sizing policy (e.g. portfolio-level gross caps across open positions) as plain functions over bars/dicts, then thread results into TradeDecision.sizing/leverage like the existing enrichers.
extension_points.notes: Keep pure-core + thin-async-fetcher split; RESEARCH.md caveats (alpha decay, vol-target=drawdown control) are encoded policy.

extension_points.how: All new execution behavior is tested by replacing _build_clients with fakes; all provider behavior via respx/monkeypatch; asyncio_mode=auto; zero network.

conventions: DISCLAIMER everywhere: every user-facing output carries core/disclaimer.py text — TradeDecision/SectorScan/MarketScreen all have a disclaimer field, every MCP prompt ends with the not-investment-advice reminder; informational/educational only (CONTRACT §2.5, §8.6 of plan.md).
CLI output is strictly ASCII (CONTRACT §10.2) so it prints on any console.

Keyless-first: every API key is optional; a missing key raises MissingApiKey at fetch time and the registry silently falls through the capability chain (never trips the circuit breaker); the entire crypto data track and paper_pairs are fully keyless.

Import safety: importing any module never hits the network and never raises on a missing key or missing optional lib; yfinance/pandas/ta are lazy-imported inside function bodies, claude_agent_sdk goes through servers/_sdk.py shims, hyperliquid-python-sdk (+eth_account) is imported lazily only inside execution.hyperliquid._build_clients; test_imports.py enforces this.

Testnet-locked execution: only Settings.hyperliquid_testnet_url exists — there is NO mainnet base-URL field anywhere in the code and no env var or argument may route a signed order at real funds; any extension must preserve this (CONTRACT §17.1).

Secret-safe: signing key only from MCP_HL_PRIVATE_KEY; core/redact.py masks any 64-hex private key (40-hex addresses stay readable); every execution error routes through redact_secrets; key never logged.
Execution tools are LIVE by default (real testnet orders, no dry-run gate) — §17.1.

MCP tool return shape is exactly {"content":[{"type":"text","text":"<json-encoded string>"}]} built via servers/_common.text_result; tools are deterministic and never call a model — AI reasoning is the MCP host's model via prompts (no ANTHROPIC_API_KEY billed).

Strict acyclic layering: Layer 2 -> Layer 1 -> Layer 0, never upward; no server imports another server except synthesis (read-only logic functions only); debate.py/crypto.py import server logic functions, never MCP wiring; execution/ isolated from providers/.
Frozen equity capability vocabulary (§4); new asset classes add NEW capability keys (as §16.1 did) rather than touching equity chains.

Tests are deterministic and offline (pytest, asyncio_mode=auto, no network): respx/monkeypatch for HTTP, _build_clients seam for the HL SDK, get_registry() monkeypatch for servers; run .venv\Scripts\python.exe -m pytest -q.
Python 3.11+, async-first I/O (sync libs wrapped in asyncio.to_thread), type hints on public functions, httpx.AsyncClient for REST, ruff line-length 100.
Tolerant fan-out: one failed symbol/capability becomes an errors/_error entry, never aborts a sweep; bounded semaphores (5 deep / 8 prefilter) protect free-tier rate budgets.

Order-placement hard backstops must be kept: coin validated against info.meta(), $10 min notional, szDecimals size rounding, 5-sig-fig price format, wrong-side-of-mid trigger rejection, TP/SL as exchange-side OCO (positionTpsl grouping).
gaps: No scheduler/daemon/loop anywhere: every surface (MCP tools, prompts, CLI) is one-shot request-driven; the only persistent-ish hook is servers/orchestration.py check_alerts (equity congress/report deltas, pull-based, console/file/webhook sinks). An always-on swarm needs an external scheduler and a crypto-side alert/monitor path that do not exist.

No local trade state: no trade journal, decision history, realized P&L tracking, or paper-performance ledger — the exchange is the only state store; nothing reconciles fills/liquidations/funding accrual over time or triggers a re-decide on regime change. Exchange-side OCO TP/SL is the only unattended protection.

Hyperliquid credentials are currently NOT configured anywhere verifiable: MCP_HL_PRIVATE_KEY / MCP_HL_ACCOUNT_ADDRESS are absent from .env and from process/User/Machine env scopes (presence-only check). An always-on process needs a credential provisioning story, and if the key is an agent wallet, MCP_HL_ACCOUNT_ADDRESS must be set to the master or all reads look empty (§17.5).

Single-account, single-key model: no multi-agent identity, per-agent budgets, or multi-wallet orchestration; agent wallets cannot move funds (transfers rejected with 'Must deposit before performing actions'), so collateral management requires the master key or manual UI action — a hard constraint for autonomous fund management.

No cross-position risk supervisor: leverage_plan is per-trade; the portfolio builder is equity-oriented; there is no gross-exposure governor across simultaneously open perp positions, no max-open-positions limit, no drawdown kill-switch, no daily loss cap — all required for a swarm placing many orders.

Execution layer is synchronous REST wrapped in asyncio.to_thread with no websocket subscriptions (fills, order updates, prices) and no documented rate limiting/retry/idempotency on the write path (ProviderRegistry's rate/circuit machinery covers only the read path) — concurrent swarm agents could collide or duplicate orders.

The documented 'best' decision (bull/bear/judge debate) requires an MCP host model in the loop — MCP sampling is unimplemented by hosts, so there is no headless way to run the debate; an autonomous swarm can only use the AI-free quant baseline (decide/decide_crypto) unless it drives a host programmatically.

Caching/concurrency are single-process: L1 in-memory cache per process, L2 disk JSON with no documented cross-process locking; multiple always-on agents sharing MCP_CACHE_DIR and free-tier rate budgets is unspecified.

Testnet-only is a non-negotiable spec invariant (§17.1: no mainnet field anywhere) — the swarm vision can never touch real funds without amending CONTRACT.md; equally, 'live by default, no dry-run' means any swarm bug places real testnet orders immediately.

No backtest/validation for the crypto derivatives signals (backtest.py covers only equity price-history long/flat); no mechanism to measure whether the engine's paper trades are actually profitable before scaling a swarm on it.

CLAUDE.md's four memory files (trading guide, execution layer, crypto expansion, architecture under ~/.claude/projects/C--Users-persi-Desktop-MakeCrazyPenny/memory/) do not exist on disk — the operational runbook the docs say to 'read first' is missing; the existing project dir C--Users-persi-Desktop-MCPenny has no memory/.

Funding-cost and liquidation figures are estimates (isolated-margin, ignores fee/maintenance tiers) and the data path has soft spots an unattended system must tolerate: Binance HTTP-451 geo-block from US IPs (auto-falls to Bybit), per-symbol funding intervals (4h/1h), and a missing funding rate that raises rather than reads 0.
